next_step_text: Confidence 10% 18th of June 2026 - waiting to get that info from PMG for both PO creation and invoice management from last demo session and since it is Ariba replacement and might be a direct materials heavy sourcing given their industry we need to find strong business use cases as the existing it challenging for Zycus , i'll be joining next session once we get our technical response for better ev

our_open_deliverables.text: Share presentation deck from 22 Apr discovery call with Puneet and team
Share contact details and slide deck from 30 Apr demo with meeting participants
Verify and update buyer on roadmap for historical supplier auto-suggestion feature
Investigate feasibility of configuring a 'power user' role for supplier admin on the portal and report back
Provide more details on the system's capabilities to refer to a catalog and pricing for contracts in the next meeting

buyer_dependent_items.text: Complete internal alignment between IT and Business teams and deliver shortlist decision
Provide demo date options for follow-up session (after Eid break)

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the incumbent being actively replaced due to complexity and integration pain, confirmed eliminated as of 22 Apr discovery call. Four other vendors (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER) are in the RFP per Salesforce Competitors__c, but none have been mentioned, praised, or preferred by the buyer in any call or Next Step through 15 Jun 2026. No evidence of any rival being ahead. Zycus differentiation (UX simplicity, SAP integration ease, modular AI roadmap) aligns strongly with buyer's stated pain and criteria. Single strongest current threat: unknown—competitive intelligence is a blind spot heading into shortlist decision due end-June 2026. Risk: we are pricing and shortlisting blind without knowing how the four other vendors compare on buyer's weighted criteria.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: They use SAP Ariba for S2P, find it too complex and not user-friendly for a 'smaller nation'; integration with SAP is painful despite both being SAP products. Ariba–SAP integration is 'too complex' and they have to act as mediator between two SAP entities.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 22 Apr 2026, Puneet Agnihotri; SF Next Step 22 Apr
competitors.how_we_win: Simpler UX and SAP integration (vs Ariba's own painful integration) plus modular roadmap; position Zycus as the anti-Ariba on complexity and make SAP S/4HANA migration support the Phase 1 centerpiece.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: No buyer mention in calls; assume active in RFP. Differentiate on SAP integration depth (Coupa's ERP connectors are generic) and modular AI roadmap (Merlin Intake on Teams, agentic negotiation). Need competitive intel before pricing.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: No buyer mention in calls; assume active in RFP. Differentiate on UX simplicity (GEP can be heavy) and free supplier portal (GEP charges vendor access fees). Need competitive intel before pricing.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: No buyer mention in calls; assume active in RFP. Differentiate on AI/agentic roadmap (iValua's AI is nascent) and simpler UX. Need competitive intel before pricing.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.how_we_win: No buyer mention in calls; assume active in RFP. Differentiate on SAP integration and AI roadmap. Need competitive intel before pricing.

champion_strength.source: MEDDPICC 2.0 Champion 23 Apr; Avoma 22 Apr, 30 Apr, 15 Jun
champion_strength.summary: Champion is Anand Singh (IT Director), project owner and recommendation-driver to CEO/CPO. Strength: developing to strong. Anand is engaged (present 22 Apr, 30 Apr, 15 Jun), drove discovery, and committed to arranging EB presentation post-procurement acceptance. At risk: Anand stated 'I will not push Zycus if procurement doesn't like it', making Dominic Xavier (Head of Procurement) acceptance a mandatory gate. Dominic was present 30 Apr but not logged as contact role and has not taken visible advocacy role. Recommended move: secure Dominic's explicit endorsement to solidify procurement-side championship and de-risk Anand's advocacy.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is confirmed as CEO and CPO (final approval authority), per MEDDPICC 2.0 (23 Apr): 'Final approval must go to CEO and CPO; they will act based on Anand's recommendation.' Budget owner: current Ariba budget will be diverted to Zycus; incremental setup cost requires separate approval (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). However, neither CEO nor CPO has been engaged directly; no calls, no contact roles, no named individuals. The 22 Apr discovery call named the 'Director IT Systems (Main decision maker)' and Puneet Agnihotri (Head of Proc Excellence) as the working-level decision drivers, and MEDDPICC 2.0 states Anand will 'arrange a meeting where you present to CEO and CPO in Kuwait to give a push' after procurement recommends Zycus. Status: EB is identified and mapped at title level, but passive (not yet engaged). Risk: heading into pricing gate (due end-June per 15 Jun call) with no direct EB validation of budget authority, pricing tolerance, or commercial constraints. Recommended move: secure Anand-led CEO/CPO executive briefing before indicative pricing submission.

recommended_moves: Deliver PMG functional response on PO creation and invoice management for direct materials sourcing with steel-industry business cases.
Ask Anand how the five RFP vendors compare on weighted criteria and what concerns exist with each before submitting indicative pricing.
Co-author ROI business case with Anand quantifying Ariba integration cost, procurement capacity reclaimed, and S/4HANA migration risk mitigation.
